title: Green Wing
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Youmay|the individual [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]|[[Green Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Green Squadron]], the [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wing]] unit with which this pilot fought during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]}} {{Nickname}} {{Update|[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 1]]}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image= |name="Green Wing" |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], over [[Endor/Legends|Endor]][[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode VI: Return of the Jedi'' novelization]] |species= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]'' |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] **[[Green Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Green Squadron]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Moving into position.|Green Wing|}} '''Green Wing''' was the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] of a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Pilot/Legends|member]] of [[Green Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Green Squadron]], a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptor]] [[Squadron/Legends|unit]] that [[Starfighter combat/Legends|fought]] during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. During the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet's]] engagement with the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] above the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], Green Wing flew into [[Space warfare/Legends|combat]] alongside [[Gold Leader/Legends|Gold Leader]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], [[Red Leader/Legends|Red Leader]] [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], and [[Blue Leader/Legends|Blue Leader]] [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Merrick Simms]] in an attack on the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] [[Pride of Tarlandia/Legends|main communications ship]], which had been completely [[Sensor jammer/Legends|jamming]] the Rebel Fleet. As the four pilots moved in on the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], Green Wing's [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] was hit by enemy fire, and he crashed into the communications ship's forward batteries. This sacrifice enabled Calrissian and Antilles to destroy the [[Warship/Legends|warship]] though Simms was [[Death/Legends|killed]] as well and stop the Imperials' [[Jammer/Legends|jamming]] of the Rebel [[Flotilla/Legends|flotilla]]. The Alliance forces would go on to destroy the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] and win the battle, despite many casualties. ==Biography== [[File:Arvel-crynyd.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Arvel Crynyd, commander of Green Squadron]] {{Quote|I'm losing power!''"
"''Get clear, you're going to blow!|Green Wing and Merrick Simms|}} Though the designation "Green Wing" could be used to refer to [[Green Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Green Squadron]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptor]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]]. as a whole, it was given as a [[Communications net|comm-net]] [[Callsign/Legends|designation]] to a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] serving as the second-in-command of Green Squadron under [[Green Leader/Legends|Green Leader]] [[Arvel Crynyd/Legends|Arvel Crynyd]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' When [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Bothan Spynet/Legends|spies]] brought the [[Death Star II plans|knowledge]] of a [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] in the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]] to [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]], Alliance [[Chief of State (Alliance)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] announced her decision to [[Battle of Endor/Legends|attack]] the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] during the final stage of its construction—while [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], ruler of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], was aboard the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. A [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel armada]], including Green Wing and his squadron mates, commanded by the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] from the [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']], would attack the Death Star while an Alliance [[Endor strike team/Legends|strike team]], led by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], attempted to destroy the [[Endor shield generator bunker|bunker]] on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] that produced the Death Star's protective [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]].{{Film|VI}} Green Wing and the Alliance Fleet emerged from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] only to find that the Death Star's shield was still active, and that the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], led by the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], was waiting for them. As the two armadas engaged each other, the Rebels learned that the Death Star's [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] was active when it destroyed the [[MC80 Liberty Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|Star Cruiser]] [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']]. General [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], commander of the Rebel [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] forces as [[Gold Leader/Legends|Gold Leader]] in the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], realized that the Alliance Fleet wouldn't stand a chance against a fully-operational Death Star. He informed Ackbar that their best bet for survival laid in engaging the Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] at point-blank range while they awaited the destruction of the shield generator protecting the battlestation. As the battle progressed, Green Wing [[Starfighter combat/Legends|flew]] alongside Lando Calrissian, [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] [[Red Leader/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], and [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]] [[Blue Leader/Legends|Leader]] [[Merrick Simms]]''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' in an attack against the Empire's [[Pride of Tarlandia/Legends|main communications ship]], which had been [[Sensor jammer/Legends|jamming]] the Alliance Fleet's [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] and thus preventing them from knowing whether or not the shield protecting the Death Star was active. The communications ship had been damaged in an attack by a Rebel [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], but the Imperial [[Destroyer/Legends|destroyer]] had managed to obliterate its attacker, and its crew had begun repairs. The Rebel pilots, knowing that the Imperial [[Warship/Legends|warship]] could become fully operational again if they did not strike immediately, moved to intercept their target, flying in a manner that made them virtually invisible to the communications ship's personnel. [[File:Awing-TSWA.png|thumb|left|250px|An RZ-1 A-wing interceptor, the type of starfighter flown by Green Squadron at Endor]] Calrissian ordered Green Wing and the rest of the group to increase the strength of their forward deflector shields, while Antilles, in his [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]], led the group in closing their formation and diving toward the destroyer's axis at a ninety-degree angle before leveling out slightly more than fifteen meters above the ship's hull and cruising along to reach their target. Immediately, they began to draw fire from the warship's batteries, and before he could coordinate with the other fighters in launching their assault, Green Wing was hit by enemy fire. With his starfighter critically damaged and on the verge of exploding, he steered his craft directly into the communications ship's forward weapons. The resulting explosion ripped through the destroyer's port side. Green Wing's sacrifice enabled the three pilots to fly into the communications ship's cargo bay, where its main [[Reactor/Legends|reactors]] were located. The trio managed to score a direct hit on the [[Power generator/Legends|power generators]], and the resulting explosions ripped apart the destroyer. Calrissian and Antilles successfully escaped the inferno, but Simms did not, as his fighter was gripped by the shock wave and thrown into the hull of a smaller Imperial warship—which was then also destroyed. With the communications ship gone, the Imperials' jamming of the Rebel Fleet ceased, but to their horror, they discovered that the deflector shield enveloping the Death Star was still active. Eventually, Solo's strike team managed to destroy the shield generator bunker on Endor, which enabled Calrissian and Antilles to lead Rebel fighters into the Death Star's superstructure and take out its main reactor, destroying the battlestation and securing a victory for the Alliance. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Thanks.|Merrick Simms, acknowledging Green Wing's sacrifice|}} Green Wing was a Rebel pilot who served as Arvel Crynyd's second-in-command in Green Squadron. As the squadron's second-in-command, he assumed a leadership role within the squadron. During the Battle of Endor, Green Wing participated in an attack on the Imperial communications ship alongside Calrissian, Antilles, and Simms. Green Wing's fighter was heavily damaged by enemy fire during their attack run, but he was able to regain enough control of his craft to steer it directly into the communications ship's forward batteries, sacrificing himself. In doing so, a hole opened in the destroyer's hull, which allowed the three other pilots to maneuver their craft into the warship's interior and ultimately take down the destroyer, eliminating the Imperials' jamming of the Rebel Fleet and ultimately giving the Rebels an advantage in the remainder of the battle. ==Behind the scenes== This otherwise-unidentified pilot, known by his squadron call sign "Green Wing," first appeared briefly in the [[1983]] [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|novelization]] [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], in which he is shown participating in a Rebel attack on the Imperial communications ship during the Battle of Endor. Though this character did not appear in the film, he was given entries in all three editions of ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]'', the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' published in [[1998]], and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' published in [[2008]]. His entry in the original ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]'' confirmed that this pilot was male, something not mentioned in the novelization. ===The other Green Wing=== {{Quote|Firing proton torpedoes.|Another pilot designated Green Wing, during the attack on the ''Executor''|}} In the ''Return of the Jedi'' novelization, another character designated "Green Wing" appears during the final assault on the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] in the closing acts of the Battle of Endor, some time after the initial Green Wing's death. This second Green Wing plays somewhat of an important role; he accompanies Green Leader in attacking the bridge of the Imperial flagship. His one line of dialogue, along with the following text, seems to indicate that his proton torpedoes are responsible for the destruction of the ''Executor''{{'s}} bridge rather than Green Leader's suicidal tactic shown in the film. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|''Star Wars: Return of the Jedi'' novelization]] {{Ab|Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 1|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 1]] {{Imo}} ==Sources== *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Green Squadron personnel]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Squadron callsigns]]
